edges.evidence_quote: On December 2, 1971, Cornell Benjamin, known as "Black Benjie", a former heroin addict turned community activist who had joined the Bronx gang the Ghetto Brothers and become their minister of peace, led a delegation to meet with members of rival gangs the Mongols, the Savage Skulls, and the Seven Immortals in order to negotiate a truce.
edges.context: Ghetto Brothers attempted to negotiate a truce with Mongols.

edges.evidence_quote: On December 2, 1971, Cornell Benjamin, known as "Black Benjie", a former heroin addict turned community activist who had joined the Bronx gang the Ghetto Brothers and become their minister of peace, led a delegation to meet with members of rival gangs the Mongols, the Savage Skulls, and the Seven Immortals in order to negotiate a truce.
edges.context: Ghetto Brothers attempted to negotiate a truce with Savage Skulls.

edges.evidence_quote: On December 2, 1971, Cornell Benjamin, known as "Black Benjie", a former heroin addict turned community activist who had joined the Bronx gang the Ghetto Brothers and become their minister of peace, led a delegation to meet with members of rival gangs the Mongols, the Savage Skulls, and the Seven Immortals in order to negotiate a truce.
edges.context: Ghetto Brothers attempted to negotiate a truce with Seven Immortals.

edges.evidence_quote: It took place on December 8, 1971, and was attended by two hundred representatives from dozens of Bronx gangs, including the Black Pearls, Black Spades, Dirty Dozens, Dutchmen, Latin Spades, Liberated Panthers, Magnificent Seven, Peacemakers, Royal Javelins, Savage Skulls, Seven Immortals, Turbans, and Young Sinners, together with police and city officials.
edges.context: Ghetto Brothers called a peace meeting attended by Black Pearls.

edges.evidence_quote: Only two women were permitted inside—the presidents of the all-girl gangs, the Alley Cats and the Savage Sisters—and they were seated in the last row, behind the warlords.
edges.context: Ghetto Brothers allowed the president of Alley Cats to attend the peace meeting.
